source_claim: Court specialists compare southern stone and Kāviri consecration with Himalayan stone and Ganges consecration for the Pattini image
Senguttuvan transforms the memorial decision into a northern royal expedition framed through Vañci victory traditions
Ministers use public proclamation and the known presence of foreign spies to transmit the expedition’s demand to northern rulers
A learned court expert identifies the auspicious moment and the king orders sword and parasol turned north
The expedition includes the five great councils eight bodies and administrative accounting ethical and strategic specialists
Large-scale food provision is assigned to the army leadership before departure
Senguttuvan receives sacred honours associated with the red-haired deity and carries them as part of departure ritual
Poets heralds bards elephant commanders cavalry leaders and warriors publicly praise the departing king

zone_or_system: VAN-Z07 military march and Nilagiri camp
source_claim: The army moves with elephants horses chariots and commanders before establishing a guarded camp on the Nilagiri heights
Descending ascetics advise the king to protect learned ritual communities living in the Himalayan region
The Hundred Kannar offer cooperation and undertake to prepare a large fleet for crossing the Ganges
Tribute goods and written royal documents are handled by designated scribal officials
A named coalition of northern rulers joins Kanaka and Vijaya and confronts the Cēra army near its northern camp
Banners drums horns infantry chariots elephants horses dust wounds and bodies construct the battle as organised mass violence
Kanaka and Vijaya are captured and Senguttuvan obtains the Pattini stone from the Himalayan region
